4.html: make a mock ( Is 66:5 ). Are ye aware of the glory of Him whom you mock, by mocking His servants ("the righteous," Is 57:1 )? ( 2Ch 36:16 ). ( Psa 22:7 , 13 ; 35:21 ; La 2:16 ). not merely children of transgressors, and a seed of false parents, but of transgression and falsehood itself, utterly unfaithful to God.

10.html: the idol which they came to worship, perfumed with oil, like harlots ( Jr 4:30 ; Eze 23:16 , 40 ). So "king" means idol ( Am 5:26 ; Zep 1:5 ); (malcham meaning "king") [Rosenmuller]. Rather, the king of Assyria or Egypt, and other foreign princes, on whom Israel relied, instead of on God; the "ointment" will thus refer to the presents ( Ho 12:1 ), and perhaps the compliances with foreigners' idolatries, whereby Israel sought to gain their favor [Lowth] ( Is 30:6 ; Eze 16:33 ; 23:16 ; Ho 7:11 ). not merely to neighboring nations, but to those "far off," in search of new idols, or else alliances. the lowest possible degradation.

16.html: The nominative is, "He that trusteth in Me" ( Is 57:13 ). The believing remnant shall have every obstacle to their return cleared out of the way, at the coming restoration of Israel, the antitype to the return from Babylon ( Is 35:8 ; 40:3 , 4 ; 62:10 , 11 ). a high road before the returning Jews. Jesus had been so to the Jews, but will not be so then any longer ( 1Co 1:23 ); their prejudices shall then be taken out of the way.
